item.title: Fetterman tells those who backed Platner to "sit it out" on a possible replacement
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman condemned his colleagues who continued backing Graham Platner amid a string of scandals.

item.title: Senate Republicans say they've spoken with Mitch McConnell as questions remain over his health
item.description: Questions are growing about the health of Kentucky Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell, who has not been seen since he was hospitalized in mid-June. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Trump considering sales of U.S. F-35 fighter jets to Turkey
item.description: President Trump spent his first day at the NATO summit in Turkey by touting his relationship with the host country's president, Tayyip Erdoğan. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has the latest. Then, former U.S. Ambassador to Turkey David Satterfield joins with analysis.

item.title: Fetterman calls Graham Platner a "total dirtbag" in wake of sexual assault allegation
item.description: Democrats are pulling their endorsements of Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner after a woman he dated accused him of sexual assault. Platner denies the claim. Democratic Sen. John Fetterman of Pennsylvania joins "The Takeout" with his reaction.

item.title: USPS raising the price of a Forever stamp starting on Sunday
item.description: The cost of a first-class Forever stamp has climbed 41% since 2021, and postal officials have signaled they want prices to rise even more.

item.title: Where Graham Platner's Senate campaign stands as Democrats pull support over assault allegation
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate nominee Graham Platner's support among lawmakers is vanishing in the wake of a sexual assault allegation first reported by Politico. Platner denies the claim. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: U.S. launches strikes on Iran after commercial ship attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As many as three tankers came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continued exchanging threats, with peace talks on hold.

item.title: U.S. launches strikes against Iran over attacks on 3 ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has over the past two days attacked three commercial vessels that were traveling through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Navy identifies missing sailor after Arabian Sea emergency landing
item.description: Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao said he has directed that Cmdr. Gabriel Edwards be posthumously promoted to his selected rank of captain.

item.title: Podcast claims North Korean IT workers are infiltrating U.S. companies
item.description: Award-winning podcast "To Catch a Thief" is returning with a look at how North Korean IT workers are getting hired as remote developers in U.S. companies. Nicole Perlroth, host of the podcast, joins CBS News to discuss her reporting.

item.title: Unstable building in Midtown forces multiple evacuations, street closures
item.description: The unstable building is the former Pfizer headquarters in Midtown Manhattan that is being converted into apartments.

item.title: U.S. conducting retaliatory strikes against Iran after attacks on commercial ships
item.description: The U.S. on Tuesday launched retaliatory strikes on Iran in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins to discuss.

item.title: What we know about the unstable Midtown Manhattan building so far
item.description: Hundreds of people evacuated a section of Midtown Manhattan on Tuesday morning after firefighters found buckling columns and sagging floor conditions in a 37-story building under construction. Officials said there were no injuries. Here's what we know so far.

item.title: Unstable Midtown Manhattan building has stopped moving, officials say
item.description: People were forced to evacuate several buildings in Midtown Manhattan on Tuesday morning after bricks started falling from the 21st floor of a 37-story building. CBS News correspondent Lilia Luciano has the latest.

item.title: Latest details on calls for Graham Platner to end campaign over sexual assault allegation
item.description: Democrats are urging Graham Platner to drop out of Maine's Senate race over allegations that he raped a woman he dated five years ago while drunk. Platner calls the accusation "categorically false." Dan Merica, anchor of the Washington Post's "Early Brief" newsletter, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Ex-Bucknell University coach charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: A former Bucknell University coach has been charged in the death of a freshman football player who collapsed during practice and died in 2024.

item.title: Trump will need help from Congress to lift sanctions on Turkey, professor says
item.description: Professor Paul Poast from the University of Chicago warns that President Trump cannot lift sanctions on Turkey without congressional approval, explaining that the measures were imposed with bipartisan support after the country purchased a missile defense system from Russia.

item.title: Ford recalls 177,000 vehicles over faulty wiper, drivetrain and other issues
item.description: A slew of Ford recalls affects some new and used Mustang, Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid and Explorer Hybrid vehicles, according to a federal safety watchdog.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect changed clothes before shooting, agent says
item.description: An investigator testified that a video shows Tyler Robinson going over a railing onto a rooftop, crouching down and running to a site overlooking where Charlie Kirk was speaking​.

item.title: Trump weighs allowing Turkey to have F-35s as NATO summit begins
item.description: Amid simmering tensions between the U.S. and some NATO allies over Iran and Greenland, President Trump is tightening bonds with Turkey.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: How much interest can a 3-year CD account earn now?
item.description: A 3-year CD account will result in a big return (and extended protection through today's economic volatility).

item.title: Prosecutors in LA announce charges against Indian syndicate "Bishnoi"
item.description: An indictment announced in Los Angeles accuses Lawrence Bishnoi and another defendant of ordering the killing of "H.S.N." — the apparent initials for Hardeep Singh Nijjar​, who was gunned down​ on June 18, 2023, near a Sikh temple in Surrey, British Columbia​.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Democrats question Trump-linked businesses over IRS settlement's implications
item.description: Senate Democrats including Elizabeth Warren, Ron Wyden, and Chuck Schumer are pressing businesses linked to President Trump about his settlement with the IRS barring the agency from pursuing certain tax claims against him, questioning whether it covers affiliated firms. Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: Mexico probing possible U.S. violation in drug lord capture: "Someone lied"
item.description: Mexico said it is investigating whether its sovereignty was violated by the United States in the 2024 capture of drug lord Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada.

item.title: Court expected to hear key recorded statement at Charlie Kirk murder hearing
item.description: Charlie Kirk's alleged killer, Tyler Robinson, is back in a Utah court for a preliminary hearing. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more details.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel expected to say Israel needs "significant changes" in speech
item.description: Rahm Emanuel​ is viewed as a potential Democratic presidential candidate and has been a longtime defender of Israel.

item.title: Trump meeting with NATO leaders including Erdoğan, Zelenskyy
item.description: President Trump is meeting with various world leaders at the NATO summit in Turkey, including Turkish President Recep Erdoğan and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and former CIA officer Elliot Ackerman comment on the political agendas for Mr. Trump's meetings.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "This Land Is Your Land"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. In celebration of the Fourth of July, here's Punch Brothers performing their cover of Woody Guthrie's classic song, "This Land Is Your Land."
